name: Fish Tacos
description: Light and flavorful fish tacos with crispy seasoned fish, tangy slaw, and creamy chipotle sauce served in warm corn tortillas.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together mayonnaise, sour cream, minced chipotle peppers, and juice of 1 lime to make the chipotle sauce. Refrigerate until ready to use.
In a medium bowl, combine shredded green and red cabbage, chopped cilantro, minced jalapeño, and juice of 1 lime. Toss well and season with salt. Set aside.
Pat fish fillets dry with paper towels. In a small bowl, combine chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper. Rub seasoning all over the fish.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook fish for 3–4 minutes per side until cooked through and flakes easily with a fork. Break into large chunks.
Warm corn tortillas in a dry skillet or directly over a gas flame for 30 seconds per side.
Assemble tacos: place fish chunks on each tortilla, top with slaw, sliced avocado, and a drizzle of chipotle sauce. Serve with remaining lime wedges.
notes: Tilapia or mahi-mahi can be substituted for cod. For extra crunch, lightly fry the fish instead of pan-searing.

name: Greek Shrimp
description: Juicy shrimp simmered in a garlicky tomato sauce with Kalamata olives, capers, and crumbled feta cheese — a Mediterranean classic ready in under 30 minutes.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium heat. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add crushed tomatoes and cherry tomatoes. Season with oregano, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 8–10 minutes until sauce thickens slightly.
Stir in Kalamata olives and capers. Nestle shrimp into the sauce in a single layer.
Cook shrimp for 2–3 minutes per side until pink and cooked through.
Squeeze half the lemon over the dish. Top generously with crumbled feta cheese and fresh parsley.
Serve immediately with crusty bread or pita to soak up the sauce.
notes: This dish can also be finished in a 400°F oven for 8 minutes after adding the shrimp. Serve over orzo or rice for a heartier meal.

name: Lemon Scallops
description: Pan-seared sea scallops with a golden crust, finished in a bright lemon butter sauce with garlic and fresh herbs. Elegant yet incredibly simple.
instructions: Pat scallops completely dry with paper towels. Season generously on both sides with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large stainless steel or cast-iron skillet over high heat until almost smoking.
Add scallops in a single layer, ensuring they are not touching. Sear for 2 minutes without moving until a deep golden crust forms.
Flip scallops and add 2 tablespoons of butter and thyme sprigs to the pan. Baste scallops with melted butter for 1–2 minutes. Transfer scallops to a plate.
Reduce heat to medium. Add garlic to the pan and cook for 30 seconds. Add white wine and scrape up any browned bits.
Stir in remaining butter, lemon juice, lemon zest, and capers. Simmer for 1–2 minutes until sauce is slightly thickened.
Return scallops to the pan briefly to coat. Garnish with fresh parsley and serve immediately.
notes: The key to a perfect sear is ensuring scallops are completely dry and the pan is very hot. Do not overcrowd the pan — work in batches if needed.

name: Lemon Garlic Butter Shrimp
description: Succulent shrimp cooked in a rich, garlicky lemon butter sauce. Ready in just 15 minutes and perfect served over pasta, rice, or with crusty bread.
instructions: Season shrimp with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil and 2 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add shrimp in a single layer and cook for 1–2 minutes per side until pink. Remove shrimp from pan and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add remaining butter to the pan and melt. Add garlic and red pepper flakes, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in chicken broth and lemon juice. Scrape up any browned bits. Simmer for 2 minutes.
Return shrimp to the pan and toss to coat in the sauce. Add lemon zest.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve immediately over pasta, rice, or with bread.
notes: Add a splash of white wine in place of chicken broth for a deeper flavor. Do not overcook the shrimp — they should be just pink and opaque.

name: Lump Crab Cakes
description: Classic Maryland-style lump crab cakes packed with sweet crab meat, minimal filler, and a crispy golden crust. Served with a zesty remoulade sauce.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, Worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, Old Bay seasoning, beaten egg, parsley, and green onions.
Gently fold in the crab meat and panko breadcrumbs, being careful not to break up the large lumps. Season with salt and pepper.
Shape mixture into 8 crab cakes, about 1 inch thick. Place on a baking sheet, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to help them hold their shape.
Heat butter and vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook crab cakes for 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown and heated through.
Serve with lemon wedges and remoulade sauce or tartar sauce.
notes: Chilling the crab cakes before cooking is essential to prevent them from falling apart. Use the best quality lump crab meat you can find for the best results.

name: Baked Salmon
description: Perfectly baked salmon fillets with a simple garlic herb butter glaze. Flaky, moist, and on the table in under 30 minutes with minimal cleanup.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with foil and lightly grease with olive oil.
In a small bowl, whisk together melted butter, garlic, Dijon mustard, honey, and soy sauce.
Place salmon fillets skin-side down on the prepared baking sheet. Season with salt, pepper, and paprika.
Spoon the garlic butter mixture evenly over each fillet. Lay lemon slices on top.
Bake for 12–15 minutes, depending on thickness, until salmon flakes easily with a fork and is opaque throughout.
Garnish with fresh dill and parsley before serving.
notes: For medium salmon, cook to an internal temperature of 125–130°F. The honey and soy sauce create a beautiful glaze — do not skip them.

name: Cavatelli and Shrimp
description: Tender cavatelli pasta tossed with plump shrimp, garlic, cherry tomatoes, and a splash of white wine in a light olive oil sauce. A rustic Italian favorite.
instructions: Cook cavatelli in a large pot of heavily salted boiling water according to package directions until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
Season shrimp with salt and pepper. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook shrimp for 1–2 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, heat remaining olive oil over medium heat. Add garlic and red pepper flakes, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add cherry tomatoes and cook for 3–4 minutes until they begin to burst. Pour in white wine and cook for 2 minutes until slightly reduced.
Add chicken broth and butter. Stir until butter is melted and sauce is smooth.
Add drained cavatelli to the pan and toss to coat. Add pasta water as needed to loosen the sauce.
Return shrimp to the pan along with fresh basil and parsley. Squeeze lemon juice over the top and toss gently.
Serve topped with grated Parmesan.
notes: Frozen cavatelli work just as well as dried. Broccoli rabe or spinach can be added for extra vegetables.

name: Shrimp and Chicken Alfredo
description: A luxurious pasta dish combining tender shrimp and juicy chicken in a velvety homemade Alfredo sauce. Rich, creamy, and utterly satisfying.
instructions: Cook fettuccine in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water. Drain and set aside.

Season chicken pieces with Italian seasoning, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken for 5–6 minutes until golden and cooked through. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add 1 tablespoon butter. Season shrimp with salt and pepper and cook for 1–2 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add remaining butter and garlic. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in heavy cream and add cream cheese. Whisk until smooth and cream cheese is fully melted.
Stir in Parmesan cheese and nutmeg. Simmer for 3–4 minutes, stirring frequently, until sauce thickens. Season with salt and pepper.
Add cooked pasta to the sauce and toss to coat. Add pasta water as needed to thin the sauce.
Return chicken and shrimp to the pan and gently toss. Garnish with fresh parsley and extra Parmesan before serving.
notes: Use freshly grated Parmesan for the best sauce — pre-shredded cheese does not melt as smoothly. Serve immediately as the sauce thickens as it cools.

name: Best Stuffed Manicotti
description: Jumbo manicotti shells filled with a three-cheese ricotta mixture, smothered in rich marinara sauce, and baked until bubbling and golden. A true Italian comfort classic.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Cook manicotti shells in salted boiling water for 6–7 minutes, until just slightly underdone. Drain and rinse with cold water to stop cooking. Lay flat on a lightly oiled sheet.
In a large bowl, combine ricotta, 2 cups mozzarella, Parmesan, eggs, parsley, garlic, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper. Stir until well combined.
Spread about 1.5 cups of marinara sauce in the bottom of a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Transfer the ricotta filling to a large zip-lock bag and snip off one corner. Pipe filling into each manicotti shell. Alternatively, use a spoon to fill each shell.
Arrange stuffed manicotti in a single layer over the sauce. Pour remaining marinara evenly over the top.
Sprinkle remaining mozzarella over the sauce. Cover tightly with foil.
Bake covered for 30 minutes. Remove foil and bake for an additional 10 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly.
Let rest for 5 minutes. Garnish with fresh basil before serving.
notes: Slightly undercooking the shells prevents them from tearing when filling. Ground Italian sausage or beef can be added to the filling or sauce for a meat version.

name: Italian Wedding Soup
description: Hearty Italian Wedding Soup with tender mini meatballs, leafy escarole, and small pasta simmered in a rich, savory broth. Warm, comforting, and deeply satisfying.

ingredients.name: acini di pepe or orzo pasta

instructions: Make the meatballs: In a bowl, combine ground beef, pork, egg, breadcrumbs, half the Parmesan, 2 minced garlic cloves, half the parsley, Italian seasoning, nutmeg, 0.5 tsp salt, and 0.25 tsp pepper. Mix until just combined. Roll into 1-inch balls.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven or pot over medium heat. Brown meatballs in batches, turning gently, about 3–4 minutes total. Transfer to a plate (they do not need to be cooked through).
In the same pot, add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook over medium heat for 5 minutes until softened. Add remaining garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in chicken broth and bring to a boil. Season with remaining salt and pepper.
Carefully drop meatballs back into the soup. Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add pasta and cook for 8–10 minutes until tender.
Stir in escarole or spinach and cook for 2–3 minutes until wilted.
Ladle into bowls and top with remaining Parmesan and fresh parsley.
notes: The meatballs can be made ahead and frozen. For a richer soup, use homemade chicken stock. Endive or kale can be used in place of escarole.

name: Pasta alla Norma
description: A beloved Sicilian pasta dish featuring tender roasted eggplant, a simple but bold tomato sauce, fresh basil, and salty ricotta salata. A vegetarian masterpiece.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Toss eggplant cubes with 4 tablespoons olive oil, 0.75 tsp salt, and black pepper. Spread on a baking sheet in a single layer.
Roast eggplant for 25–30 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and tender. Set aside.
Meanwhile, cook pasta in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water. Drain.
Heat remaining olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add garlic and red pepper flakes, cooking for 1–2 minutes until fragrant and lightly golden.
Add crushed tomatoes, remaining salt, sugar, and a few basil leaves. Simmer for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until sauce thickens.
Add roasted eggplant to the sauce and stir gently to combine.
Add cooked pasta to the sauce, tossing to coat. Use pasta water to loosen the sauce as needed.
Serve topped with torn fresh basil and crumbled ricotta salata.
notes: Ricotta salata is traditional, but Pecorino Romano or Parmesan can be substituted. Salting and draining the eggplant before roasting is optional but reduces bitterness.

name: Mexican Rice
description: Fluffy, flavorful Mexican-style rice cooked with tomatoes, garlic, onion, and spices. The perfect side dish for tacos, enchiladas, or any Mexican meal.
instructions: Heat oil in a large, heavy-bottomed saucepan or skillet with a lid over medium-high heat.
Add dry rice and toast, stirring constantly, for 3–5 minutes until the grains turn light golden brown.
Add diced onion and cook for 2 minutes until softened. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Stir in tomato sauce, drained diced tomatoes, cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Pour in chicken broth and stir to combine. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover tightly, and cook for 18–20 minutes until liquid is absorbed and rice is tender.
Remove from heat. Scatter frozen peas over the top, cover, and let steam for 5 minutes.
Fluff rice with a fork. Stir in peas and garnish with fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice before serving.
notes: Toasting the rice before adding liquid is the secret to fluffy, separate grains. Use vegetable broth to keep this dish fully vegetarian.
